ideation: This animation sequence will simulate the joyful, upward motion of riding a carousel horse.  The robot will mimic the rising and falling motion of a rider, emphasizing the cyclical nature of the carousel.  Both arms will move in a complementary, mirroring fashion. The movement will be slightly exaggerated to give the animation an expressive quality. I will use the examples to ensure that joint movements are within the servo's capabilities and the movements are fluid and natural-looking. Frame 1: Both arms are held slightly out to the sides, mimicking a rider holding on. Frame 2: The arms move upward, simulating the horse rising. Frame 3: The arms reach their peak, then begin to drop slightly. Frame 4: Arms are back down to slightly above neutral position. Frame 5: Arms return to the starting position. This cyclical nature of the movement will create a sense of motion and excitement.

frames.beat: arms held out slightly, ready for the ride
arms lift upwards, the carousel begins its ascent
arms reach the peak, a moment of exhilaration
arms begin to fall gently with the horse's dip
arms return to the starting position, cycle complete
